rationale: The cited 2001 paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core question because both focus on nonlinear/curvilinear effects of satisfaction and the distinction between intention and behavior outcomes. The citing paper explicitly theorizes and estimates quadratic satisfaction effects on loyalty intentions, while the cited paper reports strong nonlinearity in the satisfaction–repurchase link and contrasts satisfaction-intent versus behavior mappings. Given this conceptual overlap, this is not clearly an incidental mention and should be reviewed in second-stage judgment.
